description: After 1m-1M-1NT, bid the other minor as an artificial checkback to find major-suit fits or determine strength
purpose: After opener rebids 1NT, responder may need to explore for an 8-card major fit or determine whether to invite or bid game. NMF provides a one-round forcing mechanism to extract more information from opener.

authority.label: Bridge Guys — comprehensive NMF coverage with opener's rebid structure

teaching.tradeoff: After 1m-1M-1NT, the bid of the other minor loses its natural meaning in exchange for an artificial forcing checkback bid.
teaching.principle: After a 1NT rebid by opener, responder may still need to explore for an 8-card major fit or determine whether to invite or bid game. NMF provides a one-round forcing mechanism to extract more information.

commonMistakes.text: Do not rebid opener's minor and call it NMF.
commonMistakes.reason: The convention is always the other minor, because the opened minor stays available as a natural rebid.

commonMistakes.text: Do not use NMF without invitational-or-better values.
commonMistakes.reason: The checkback is forcing, so weak hands should sign off in responder's suit or pass one notrump instead of creating a one-round force.

commonMistakes.text: Do not rebid notrump before checking for three-card support.
commonMistakes.reason: Opener's first job after NMF is to show support for responder's major before defaulting to notrump.

explanationEntries.displayText: Responder has invitational+ values (10+ HCP)
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Not enough for NMF — need 10+ HCP

explanationEntries.displayText: Responder has game values (12+ HCP)
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Not enough for game — need 12+ HCP

clauses.rationale: concentrated values in the NMF minor

teachingLabel.name: 3♣ raise NMF minor

teachingLabel.name: 2♥ rebid own suit
teachingLabel.summary: Priority 5: rebid the opened major with 6+ card length

summaryCard.trigger: After one minor, one major, and one notrump, responder checks back with the other minor.
summaryCard.partnership: The new minor is whichever minor responder has not bid. Some partnerships treat it as invitational-or-better by default; others play strictly game-forcing or a two-way style that splits invitational and game-forcing across the two minors — confirm the agreement. The summary card shows the one-club branch.

whenToUse.gloss: Use NMF when invite-or-better values still need major support checked.
Use NMF with game-going values before choosing notrump or a major.
Use NMF with invitational values when a direct notrump invite is too blunt.

workedAuctions.label: Club opening branch uncovers a heart fit

calls.rationale: Opener starts with the minor opening.
Responder shows hearts first.
Opener rebids notrump instead of raising hearts immediately.
Responder uses the other minor as NMF to ask for more detail.
Opener shows three-card heart support with a minimum.
Responder places the contract in the newly discovered heart fit.

workedAuctions.label: Diamond opening branch signs off in invitational notrump

calls.rationale: Opener begins with the diamond opening.
Responder shows spades.
Opener rebids notrump, denying immediate spade support.
Responder checks back in the new minor.
Opener shows a minimum without a major fit.
Responder invited by asking and now signs off in two notrump.

workedAuctions.label: One-heart start uses the two-club checkback to find spades

calls.rationale: Opener starts with hearts.
Responder shows spades.
Opener limits the hand with the notrump rebid.
Responder uses the artificial two-club NMF call.
Opener jumps to show maximum values with three-card spade support.
Responder places the contract in the spade fit.

workedAuctions.label: Counter-example: weak values do not justify the forcing checkback

calls.rationale: Opener begins with one club.
Responder shows the heart suit.
Opener rebids notrump.
Responder launches NMF anyway, even though the hand lacks the values to force.

interference.reason: Opponent interference over 2♣/2♦ checkback is a partnership agreement — systems-on or penalty double; not covered here.

items.recommendation: Check back, then decide between two notrump and a partscore in the major.
items.note: Opener first shows three-card support, then the other major, then clarifies minimum notrump.

items.recommendation: Check back before placing game in three notrump or the major.
items.note: A discovered fit takes priority; without one, opener's notrump range tells responder whether to bid game.

items.recommendation: Check back before any serious slam move.
items.note: The convention confirms support or shape first, then the partnership can launch natural slam exploration.

relatedLinks.discriminator: NMF is the post-one-notrump-rebid analog of Stayman's four-card-major ask.
Bergen shows support immediately after a major opening, while NMF checks back only after opener limits the hand with one notrump.

definitions.description: Spades are at least as long as hearts
Clubs are longer than diamonds, or the minors are equal at 3-3
Diamonds are at least as long as clubs
